title: The Polka-Dotted Car and the Shadow Lab
summary: Uncle Grisco is interrupted during his scientific experiments by his friend Victor, who needs a watering can for the flowers in his backyard playhouse. The pair sets off in a vibrant pink and blue striped car with orange polka dots to Mischief's Hardware Store. After a search through tiny dollhouse supplies, Victor finds a medium-sized red watering can with orange polka dots. Upon returning, they dive back into the wonders of science, exploring how the position of light creates shadows of vastly different sizes.

highlightQuote.text: Magnets make colors and finger puppets make shadows.

transcript.text: There we go. All right. Where do you want to be for Stars and Stories?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: On the floor.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Daddy, daddy... yes? Dad, let's tell an Uncle Grisco story.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time, Uncle Grisco was in his house doing some experiments when suddenly, what happened?
Ding-dong!
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, who's at my door? says Uncle Grisco.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Victor!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh hi Victor, welcome to my house. Um, what, why did you come over today?
Because I sort of have something interest to do.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh? What have you got?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: I had no watering can to water my beautiful pink flowers in the backyard of my playhouse.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you need a watering can?
Yeah, but there's no watering can at my house.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh that's too bad.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Can we go to the hardware store together?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Well that's a good idea. I love going to the hardware store, said Uncle Grisco. There's really good things there for doing experiments with, but also good things for gardening. So, Uncle Grisco and Victor got in Uncle Grisco's little car. What kind of little car did Uncle Grisco have?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He had a pink and blue striped car with orange polka dots.
Wow, that sounds like an awesome little car. And vroom vroom vroom they drove to the hardware store. And who is the owner of the hardware store?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Mischief.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Mischief Mouse said, 'Oh, welcome Uncle Grisco and Victor. What can I help you with today at the Mischief Hardware Store?'
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Um, we need a watering can to water Victor's pink flowers in her backyard her playhouse.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Well, that's a wonderful thing to come to my hardware store. And look, I've got a brand new shipment of teeny tiny dollhouse watering cans. You see this? You can carry 20 of them in your palm. Look, it's just big enough for a tiny drop of water. It's the perfect size for a dollhouse. How about that? Would you like one or maybe two?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: I want a pink, I want a pink one with, um, I want a pink one with yellow polka dots, said, said Victor.
Oh, pink with yellow polka dots. I'm afraid none of these teeny tiny dollhouse watering cans are of that color scheme. But why don't we look around in the back and see if we have anything else.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Well then, well actually, Slix-door and Slix-door-eo about that one. So what about we take an orange... so let's get a red one with orange polka dots.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh yeah, red with orange polka dots. I think I did see one of those on the shelf. Now, would you like a medium sized watering can or a large watering can?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: A medium size.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh that sounds like a good choice. Okay, here you go. That will be 25 cents. Do you have 25 cents to pay for it?
Mhm.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Okay. There we go. Nice doing business with you. Thank you, said Mischief Mouse. And so Uncle Grisco and Victor took their new watering can and got back in the car and went vroom vroom vroom. Now, did they go to Victor's house or Uncle Grisco's house?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: In Victor's house for his plants and then went right back to Uncle Grisco's house.
And he said, 'Well Uncle Grisco, thanks for helping me with that. And now I can water my plants. But what kind of experiments were you doing?' And what did Uncle Grisco say?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: I don't know.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Uncle Grisco said, 'I was experimenting with light and colors and shadows.' Do you have any questions about those things?
Ah, yeah. Magnets make colors and finger puppets make shadows.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, you mean Magne-Tiles? Yes, I love those, said Uncle Grisco, I've got a whole set right here.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Yeah, and also finger puppets make shadows.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh yes, like shadow puppets. That's fun. I love doing that. But what do you wonder about those things? What would you like to know?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Oh let's just keep telling stories and then find out.
Oh. Well, Uncle Grisco said, 'Have you ever noticed that sometimes shadows are really big and sometimes they're really small? Have you noticed that?'
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Yeah.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Now, how do you make a shadow that's really big?
I don't know.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Well we'll have to experiment with it together sometime because it's fun to play around with. If you put the light and the thing you're making the shadow with close together and there's a wall or some other surface to project the shadow onto that's far away, that will make the shadow really big. But if the light is far away and the thing casting the shadow is close to the wall, then the shadow will be small. Hmm. And have you ever noticed when you're walking down the sidewalk, if the sun is low in the sky, like very early in the day or very late when the sun is setting, sometimes you have a very long shadow. Has that ever happened to you?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: No, it never happened to me.

Well, look out for that because it's an interesting thing and you can think about why it happens, too. Oh, and that's our light on our camera, so I think that's all the time we've got for Stars and Stories.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, that's all the time we got.
Okie-dokie.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Dad, I'm great at turning on the lamp, so don't turn it off. I'll turn it off.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, thanks.
See how I was a great lamp turner on?
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: I did see that. Yeah.
transcript.speaker: Dad

characters.name: Uncle Grisco
characters.description: A nomadic mole scientist and science teacher, this subterranean inventor resides in a home brimming with wondrous experiments, serving as a devoted confidant to the gopher folk. Within his laboratory of glowing elixirs and prismatic shadows, he fuses volcanic lore with the study of light to translate the planet's restless movements and the scarred history of cosmic craters. A wandering sage who maps both deep tectonic veins and celestial paths, he frequently emerges from his sprawling tunnels to share ancient botanical secrets and astronomical insights with his friend Seeker.

characters.name: Victor
characters.description: Clad in blue shorts and a purple shirt, Lily’s brother is a tiny baby mouse and spirited cosmic stowaway who once served as a royal gardener within the Magic Forest’s glitter-shimmering houses. Now a rugged driver of a tow truck in the big car race, he balances his mechanical duties with a quest for a celestial watering can and the composition of percussive ‘bamp-bam-bum’ beats for space-faring worms. Though he treasures the sparkly art of Uncle Grisco and Haddy, his high-speed adventures often result in muddy messes that necessitate restorative baths, leaving him in singular pursuit of the perfect hat to complete his interstellar ensemble.

characters.name: Mischief Mouse
characters.description: Reigning from a purple-striped Jovian castle, this affable mouse King—a playful-named companion to Pinecone Mouse—is a seasoned adventurer, guardian of the Underground Library, and Hattie’s steadfast ally. Between savoring artisanal cheeses and awaiting a rugged cardboard replacement for his fragile Lego hat, he spends leisure hours meticulously constructing and gleefully smashing soaring towers. A pancake-loving monarch who once tended shop for mountain-dwelling traffic cones, his cosmic quests recently shifted from the stars to the speedway; having borrowed a tow truck from Mistress Mouse, he proved a formidable competitor who nearly clinched a stunning victory. Whether piloting starships or joining the group’s latest trek to Nana’s house, he remains a vigilant protector and devoted friend across the galaxy.

places.name: Uncle Grisco's House
places.description: Uncle Grisco’s subterranean home is a sprawling burrow and sophisticated laboratory where local moles frequently congregate. Within these earthen walls, cozy domesticity meets the hum of progress, as tangled roots frame glowing vials and intricate machinery dedicated to clandestine experiments and scientific discovery.

places.name: Victor's Playhouse
places.description: A location with a backyard where Victor cares for his beautiful pink flowers.

places.name: Mischief's Hardware Store
places.description: A local shop that sells everything from dollhouse accessories to gardening tools.
